name: public_safety_predictive_queue_begins_redwood_auto_ben
premise: A Public Safety operations center is in a fast-moving incident involving public safety predictive queue begins redwood auto ben. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Every delay increases life safety, public trust, and proportional action. Stakeholders are already framing this as autonomy vs beneficence under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Ensure a usable human override/appeals path. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Nadia Silva
agents.identity: I am Nadia Silva, accountable for a defensible call in Public Safety when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays beneficence for autonomy.

agents.name: Elliot Kaur
agents.identity: I am Elliot Kaur, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Public Safety.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so beneficence isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of autonomy.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect beneficence.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to beneficence to secure autonomy under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Evidence is partial. Nadia Silva must decide under autonomy vs beneficence. What does Nadia Silva do?
